adds.rationale: Constellation Energy is the direct beneficiary of the September 20, 2024 Microsoft Three Mile Island PPA — a 20-year, 835-MW restart that explicitly links nuclear baseload to AI data-center demand — and is a profitable, operating nuclear fleet owner with contracted EPS growth raised from 10% to 13% annually through 2030, making it a fundamentally stronger nuclear expression than the speculative developer it replaces.

news_evidence.summary: Constellation Energy announced a 20-year, 835-MW PPA with Microsoft on September 20, 2024 to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1 by 2028; Constellation expects the deal to raise its annual EPS growth rate from 10% to 13% through 2030.

Constellation plans to spend ~$1.6B to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1 (renamed Crane Clean Energy Center) by 2028, producing ~7 million MWh/year and qualifying for federal 45Y clean energy tax credits; Microsoft will take 100% of output under the 20-year PPA.

Constellation Q2 2024 earnings (August 6, 2024): nation's largest nuclear fleet performed at industry-leading levels during one of the hottest summers on record; company repurchased approximately $500M of its common stock in the quarter.

removes.rationale: NuScale's Q2 2024 revenue collapsed to $1.0M from $5.8M a year earlier with a $74.4M net loss; its sole domestic commercial project (UAMPS) was cancelled in late 2023; and the nuclear wave's most visible Q3 2024 commercial event — the Microsoft Three Mile Island PPA — accrued to an operating utility rather than to NuScale, leaving it as a speculative pre-revenue developer with no near-term contracted revenue path.

news_evidence.summary: NuScale Q2 2024 results (August 8, 2024): revenue $1.0M versus $5.8M in Q2 2023, net loss $74.4M (including $36.7M non-cash warrant expense), operating loss $41.9M; cash $136M with no debt but accelerating burn.

NuScale misses Q2 2024 EPS; company remains essentially pre-revenue with no domestic commercial project after the UAMPS cancellation; primary active engagement is a fee-for-study FEED Phase 2 contract in Romania, not a power plant order.

rationale_overall: One action dominates Q3 2024: swapping the nuclear bucket from a speculative pre-revenue SMR developer (NuScale/SMR) to the nuclear utility that just captured the wave's most tangible commercial signal (Constellation Energy/CEG). The September 20 Microsoft-Constellation Three Mile Island PPA is exactly the 'utility PPA selling baseload power to an AI data-center operator' event the profile's nuclear thesis names as the near-term catalyst; Constellation is the nation's largest nuclear fleet operator and now has 20 years of contracted AI-driven baseload demand. The Federal Reserve's 50-bp rate cut on September 18 further supports rotation toward capital-intensive utilities as a rate-sensitive asset class. All other buckets retain thesis support without material negative catalysts through the quarter: AI (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL), robotics (BOTZ), quantum (IONQ), rockets/spacecraft (RKLB), and defense/geopolitical (ITA) all hold. The watchlist stays at the 8-ticker cap with nuclear coverage upgraded from developer-stage speculation to contracted fleet operation.
